title: Qwen-AEC: Instruction-tuning Large Language Models for High-Performance Building Code Analysis
task: 建筑规范文本分类与命名实体识别
taskEn: Building code text classification and named entity recognition
abstract: The architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC) domain relies heavily on complex building codes, the analysis of which is crucial but challenging to automate effectively. This study challenges the prevailing assumption that high performance on such tasks necessitates resource-intensive, domain-specific pre-training. We introduce instruction-tuning as a highly resource-efficient paradigm to adapt general-purpose large language models (LLMs) for these specialized tasks. To this end, we developed Qwen-AEC, a series of models based on the Qwen3 series, fine-tuned using meticulously constructed instruction-following datasets for building code text classification (TC) and named entity recognition (NER). Our experiments demonstrate that Qwen-AEC achieves highly competitive, SOTA-level performance, attaining weighted-F1 scores exceeding 92% for TC and macro-F1 scores exceeding 83% for NER, matching SOTA performance on TC and surpassing it on NER. Crucially, our findings provide new knowledge on the trade-offs between model scale and adaptation efficiency; even a lightweight 1.7B parameter Qwen-AEC model rivals the performance of significantly larger, specialized SOTA models. Ultimately, this study provides two key contributions to the engineering informatics field. It establishes instruction-tuning as a resource-efficient and powerful alternative to traditional, resource-intensive adaptation methods. By demonstrating this SOTA-level performance with accessible models, this study significantly lowers the barrier for the practical application of advanced LLMs in the AEC domain.
pdfFile: Chen et al. - 2026 - Qwen-AEC Instruction-tuning large language models for high-performance building code analysis
markdownFile: Chen et al. - 2026 - Qwen-AEC Instruction-tuning large language models for high-performance building code analysis
contribution: 开发了Qwen-AEC系列模型，通过指令微调而非领域预训练实现建筑规范文本分类和命名实体识别的SOTA级性能，证明轻量级1.7B参数模型即可媲美大型专用模型
methodology: 基于Qwen3系列模型，构建建筑规范领域的指令跟随数据集，将文本分类（7类：direct/indirect/method/reference/general/term/others）和命名实体识别（7类实体）统一为指令格式，使用LoRA参数高效微调技术进行指令微调，在1.7B和8B两种参数规模上进行实验
results: Qwen-AEC-8B-TC-think在文本分类上达到加权F1=92.40%，与领域专用SOTA模型（ARCBERT-Large 94.4%）差距仅2个百分点；Qwen-AEC-8B-NER在命名实体识别上达到部分匹配宏F1=83.75%，超越SOTA模型ARCBERT-Small（81.8%）；轻量级1.7B模型在两个任务上分别达到92.21%和78.06%
